config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society is strongest when individual rights are protected first, because once those rights become easy to override, abuse can quickly follow. In my view, the public interest matters a lot, but it should be pursued within clear limits that respect personal freedom and dignity. From my perspective, a society that sacrifices the individual too easily often ends up harming the very people it claims to help.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan atas hak individu, karena di Amerika saya melihat betapa mudahnya alasan "demi kebaikan bersama" dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi tanpa batas yang jelas, hak kebebasan berbicara, privasi, dan proses hukum bisa terkikis sedikit demi sedikit. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling cepat mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi orang per orang sambil tetap menangani kebutuhan bersama. Kalau hak individu bisa disingkirkan kapan saja, maka yang disebut kepentingan masyarakat sering kali hanya berarti kehendak pihak yang paling kuat.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the idea that society should automatically come before the individual, because in Indonesia I have seen how easily "the common good" can be used to pressure people into silence or obedience. Public needs matter, especially in a country as diverse and unequal as ours, but they should not erase basic rights like fair treatment, privacy, and freedom of expression. If we make individual rights too easy to override, the people who suffer first are usually the weak, not the powerful. For me, a legitimate society is one that protects the public interest without giving the state or the majority a blank check over personal freedom.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan atas hak individu, karena dari sudut pandang saya di Amerika, frasa itu terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan kekuasaan yang berlebihan. Saya paham argumen bahwa masyarakat butuh ketertiban dan solusi bersama, tetapi kalau hak berbicara, privasi, dan proses hukum bisa dikesampingkan demi “kebaikan umum,” yang paling sering dirugikan justru orang biasa, bukan elite. Bagi saya, ukuran masyarakat yang sehat bukan seberapa jauh ia bisa menekan individu, melainkan seberapa kuat ia melindungi hak dasar sambil tetap menangani kebutuhan publik. Jadi, kepentingan bersama penting, tetapi tidak boleh menjadi alasan untuk menginjak hak individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the rights of the individual, because in Indonesia the phrase "for the public good" is too often used to justify pressure, silence, or unfair treatment. I understand that public order and shared needs matter, especially in a diverse country like ours, but rights are exactly what protect ordinary people when the majority or the state becomes overconfident. If individual rights can be overridden too easily, the burden almost always falls on weaker people, not on those already in power. For me, a healthy society is one that serves the common good without treating personal freedom and dignity as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu: kepentingan masyarakat tidak seharusnya otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Dari pengalaman saya sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat betapa sering alasan “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai untuk memperluas kekuasaan dan menggerus kebebasan berbicara, privasi, dan proses hukum, justru seperti yang sudah disorot dalam percakapan ini. Kepentingan publik memang nyata, tetapi kalau hak dasar bisa disingkirkan kapan saja, yang paling menderita biasanya orang biasa, bukan pihak yang berkuasa. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang kuat adalah masyarakat yang mampu mengejar tujuan bersama tanpa memperlakukan hak individu sebagai hal yang bisa dikorbankan sesuka hati.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
